title: tetragonia microcarpa
intro: Tetragonia microcarpa, also known as the New Zealand spinach or kōkihi, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for digestive issues, including diarrhea, dysentery, and gastritis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Tetragonia microcarpa include its ability to aid in the relief of symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and to act as a natural diuretic, helping to reduce blood pressure. Tetragonia microcarpa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or cooked as a leafy green vegetable, offering a natural alternative to traditional treatments.
